original_hypothesis.title: Re-Whitelisting Market Creates Stale accCantoPerShare Without Resetting User Positions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a market is re-whitelisted, `lastRewardBlock` is reset to `block.number` but `accCantoPerShare` retains its old value. Users who had deposits before de-listing still have nonzero `user.amount` and old `rewardDebt` values. The lending market could not call `sync_ledger` during the de-listing period, so if users withdrew from the actual lending market during that period, `lendingMarketTotalBalance` and `user.amount` in LendingLedger are now stale and inconsistent with reality.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: After re-whitelisting, the user.amount in LendingLedger no longer reflects actual lending market positions. New rewards accrue based on stale, inflated total balances, diluting legitimate depositors. Users who withdrew from the actual lending market during de-listing still earn rewards post re-listing based on phantom balances.

proof_of_signal.pattern: whiteListLendingMarket only updates lastRewardBlock when re-whitelisting; accCantoPerShare, lendingMarketTotalBalance, and all userInfo entries remain unchanged

preconditions: Market was whitelisted with active users
Governance de-whitelists and later re-whitelists the market
Users interacted with the actual lending market during the de-listing period

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Only lastRewardBlock is reset on re-whitelist; all user accounting preserved
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: sync_ledger reverts during de-listing, so lending market cannot update LendingLedger state. Post re-whitelist, LendingLedger state diverges from actual market positions.
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No state reconciliation mechanism on re-whitelist, no forced sync

analysis_questions: What happens if a lending market catches sync_ledger reverts and processes user withdrawals anyway?
Can phantom balances earn and claim rewards post re-whitelisting?
Is there any mechanism for the lending market to reconcile state after re-whitelisting?
validation_requirements: Simulate de-whitelist → user withdraws from market → re-whitelist → user claims
Verify whether phantom balances accrue rewards
Check if lending market implementation handles sync_ledger reverts

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Market is de-whitelisted. User withdraws cNOTE from the actual lending market (sync_ledger reverts, ignored). Market is re-whitelisted. LendingLedger still shows user with large balance. New rewards accrue and user claims CANTO based on phantom balance they no longer have.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Users can claim rewards based on phantom balances they no longer hold
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Legitimate current depositors whose rewards are diluted by phantom positions
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Lending market must manually reconcile all user positions after re-whitelisting

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for de-whitelist → re-whitelist state consistency

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by comparing LendingLedger balances with actual market balances

validation_result.impact_summary: When a market is re-whitelisted, user positions and total balances that diverged during de-listing are not reconciled. Phantom balances earn new rewards, diluting legitimate depositors.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis correctly identifies a code defect in the re-whitelisting logic. When `whiteListLendingMarket(_market, true)` is called for a previously de-whitelisted market, only `lastRewardBlock` is reset to `block.number`. The values `accCantoPerShare`, `lendingMarketTotalBalance[_market]`, and all `userInfo[_market][*]` entries are preserved as-is. During the de-listing period, `sync_ledger` reverts because `update_market` requires `lendingMarketWhitelist[_market]` to be true (line 56). If the lending market handles this revert gracefully (e.g., try/catch), users can withdraw from the actual lending market without updating LendingLedger. After re-whitelisting, those users retain their stale `user.amount` in LendingLedger, creating phantom balances. New rewards accrue against `lendingMarketTotalBalance` (which includes phantom balances), and phantom-balance users can claim those rewards via `claim()`. There is no reconciliation mechanism, no forced re-sync, and no way for the protocol to detect or correct the divergence automatically.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Line 56 confirms `update_market` requires whitelist: `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market], "Market not whitelisted");`. Line 82 confirms `sync_ledger` calls `update_market(lendingMarket)`, so it reverts for de-whitelisted markets. Lines 119-125 show `whiteListLendingMarket` only resets `lastRewardBlock` on re-whitelist, preserving all other state. Lines 96-106 show `claim()` calculates rewards from the preserved `user.amount` and updated `accCantoPerShare`, allowing phantom-balance users to claim rewards they didn't earn.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: On re-whitelisting, only lastRewardBlock is reset. accCantoPerShare, lendingMarketTotalBalance, and all userInfo entries are preserved. Any state divergence that occurred during de-listing (when sync_ledger was blocked) persists permanently with no reconciliation.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
function update_market(address _market) public {
    require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market], "Market not whitelisted");
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This whitelist check causes sync_ledger to revert during de-listing, preventing the lending market from updating LendingLedger when users deposit/withdraw from the actual market. This is the root cause that allows state divergence.

After re-whitelisting, claim uses the stale user.amount (phantom balance) with the newly accumulated accCantoPerShare to compute rewards. A user who withdrew from the actual lending market during de-listing can still claim rewards based on a balance they no longer hold.

calling_sequence: 1. Market is whitelisted. User deposits 100 cNOTE → sync_ledger sets user.amount=100, lendingMarketTotalBalance=100
2. Governance calls whiteListLendingMarket(_market, false) → market de-whitelisted

3. User withdraws 100 cNOTE from actual lending market. Lending market calls sync_ledger → update_market reverts at line 56 ('Market not whitelisted'). If lending market catches revert, withdrawal proceeds without updating LendingLedger
4. Governance calls whiteListLendingMarket(_market, true) → lastRewardBlock = block.number. user.amount still 100, lendingMarketTotalBalance still 100, accCantoPerShare unchanged
5. Blocks pass. Anyone calls update_market → new rewards accrue: accCantoPerShare increases based on lendingMarketTotalBalance=100 (which includes phantom balance)

6. User calls claim(_market) → accumulatedCanto = 100 * new_accCantoPerShare / 1e18, cantoToSend = accumulatedCanto - rewardDebt. User receives CANTO rewards for phantom 100 cNOTE they don't actually hold
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The defect path is: whiteListLendingMarket(false) blocks sync_ledger via update_market require at line 56 → user positions diverge from reality → whiteListLendingMarket(true) at lines 119-125 only resets lastRewardBlock → claim() at lines 96-106 uses stale user.amount to compute rewards → phantom-balance user extracts unearned rewards.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided. The defect is in the core contract logic, not dependent on deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is valid. The critical dependency is whether the lending market handles the `sync_ledger` revert during de-listing. If the lending market uses try/catch or any revert-handling pattern around the `sync_ledger` call, users can withdraw from the lending market without LendingLedger being updated. Even if the lending market doesn't handle the revert (causing user withdrawals to also revert), this is still a code defect because: (1) there's no documentation requiring lending markets to make sync_ledger reverts fatal, (2) the LendingLedger contract has no mechanism to handle or reconcile state after re-whitelisting regardless of lending market behavior. Even in the case where the lending market does prevent withdrawals during de-listing, new deposits after re-whitelisting still accrue based on the stale total balance (if other users deposited elsewhere, the proportions are wrong).
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. Users with phantom balances earn rewards they're not entitled to, diluting legitimate depositors' rewards. The magnitude depends on how much position divergence occurred during de-listing. In the worst case, a user could retain a large phantom balance and continuously drain CANTO rewards. The attack requires governance action (de-whitelist then re-whitelist) which limits the attack surface, but the defect is real and could result in meaningful reward misallocation.
recommended_mitigations: Reset all market accounting state (accCantoPerShare, secRewardsPerShare, lendingMarketTotalBalance) when re-whitelisting a market, and require all users to re-sync their positions
Add an emergency claim function that works for de-whitelisted markets (allowing users to claim before state diverges)
Implement a reconciliation mechanism that allows governance or the lending market to batch-update user positions after re-whitelisting
Consider allowing sync_ledger to work even for de-whitelisted markets (skip reward accrual but still track balance changes), preventing state divergence
Add a separate function that updates balances without requiring whitelist status, callable only by the lending market
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LendingLedger with mock GaugeController. 2. Whitelist a mock lending market. 3. Have user deposit 100 cNOTE via sync_ledger. 4. Set rewards for current epoch. 5. Advance blocks, verify rewards accrue. 6. De-whitelist the market. 7. Verify sync_ledger reverts for the market. 8. Re-whitelist the market. 9. Set rewards for new epoch. 10. Advance blocks. 11. Have user call claim() and verify they receive rewards based on their stale 100 cNOTE balance, even though they 'conceptually' withdrew (LendingLedger was never informed).
uncertainties: Whether specific lending market implementations handle sync_ledger reverts gracefully or treat them as fatal - this determines real-world exploitability but not the existence of the defect
Whether governance would realistically de-whitelist and re-whitelist a market without manual intervention to reconcile state
additional_testing_needed: Test the exact reward amounts a phantom-balance user can claim after re-whitelisting
Test whether a user who was fully withdrawn before de-listing but re-deposits after re-whitelisting has correct accounting
Test interaction between stale lendingMarketTotalBalance and new deposits after re-whitelisting (reward dilution effects)
Examine actual lending market implementations to determine if sync_ledger reverts are caught or propagated
